explanation: This command matches the Sigma rule by using icacls.exe with the /deny flag and the *S-1-1-0 SID, which aligns with the detection logic. It is a direct representation of the rule's intent.

This command uses an escape character (^) before the /deny flag to simulate a safe mutation technique. The command remains functionally equivalent but demonstrates how insertion of escape characters can alter the command's appearance.
This command reorders the (OI)(CI) flags to (CI)(OI), which is accepted by icacls as the order of ACE flags does not affect functionality. This demonstrates reordering as a safe mutation technique.
This command omits the quotes around the file path, which is allowed if the path does not contain spaces. This demonstrates omission as a safe mutation technique.
This command uses command substitution to represent the ACE flags dynamically. This is a safe test-only technique for simulating recoding or encoding.
